chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD C.P.No.D- 67 of 2019 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 1. For orders on office objection 2. For hearing of MA-272/19 3. For hearing of main case. 14.11.2019. Mr. Ayaz Hussain Tunio, advocate holding brief for Mr. Khadim Hussain Soomro advocate for petitioners. M/s. Muhammad Ismail Bhutto, A.A.G Sindh and Shahid Ahmed Shaikh, D.P.G for the State. = By way of instant petition, the petitioners have sought for quashment of FIR(s) registered against him. The statements filed on behalf of respondents No.2 to 7, those are taken on record. It is stated by learned A.A.G Sindh that the reports u/s 173 Cr.P.C in FIR(s) which are sought to be quashed by the petitioners have already been submitted before the Courts having jurisdiction and after submission of challan no FIR could legally be quashed. Learned counsel who is holding brief for learned counsel for the petitioners when was confronted with the statement of learned A.A.G Sindh, stated that he would not press the disposal of instant petition on merits, if the petitioners are permitted to seek their acquittal in accordance with law by making application under Section 249-A/265-K Cr.P.C before the Court(s) having jurisdiction. Order accordingly. 2 Needless to state if, an application u/s 249-A/265-K Cr.P.C is moved for acquittal of the petitioners, then it would be disposed of by learned trail Court strictly in accordance with law on its own merits. JUDGE JUDGE Ahmed/Pa,
